### FORMAT OF PROOF LINES
Each proof line must be structured like: '<line_number>. <formula> <origin>'
- <line_number>: sequential integer, always ending with a dot ('1.', '2.', '3.', ...)
- <formula>: the formula derived on that line
- <origin>: either 'pre' (premise), 'hip' (hypothesis) or '<rule_name> <line_refs>'
  - <rule_name> and <line_refs>: 
    - &e (1 line)  
    - &i (2 lines)  
    - ->e (2 lines)  
    - ->i (1 range)  
    - ~e (2 lines)  
    - ~i (1 range)  
    - |e (1 line + 2 ranges)  
    - |i (1 line)  
    - @e (1 line)  
    - raa (1 range)  
    - copie (1 line)  
  - <line_refs>: Must be comma-separated line numbers or ranges. Ranges denote subproofs (e.g., `->i 8-12`, `|e 4, 5-9, 10-14`)

### SUBPROOFS / HYPOTHESES
Use subproofs for hypotheses. Use curly braces '{' and '}' to open and close subproofs. 
Place `{` immediately after the line number (e.g. '8.{ A|B hip') and `}` at the end of the final line of the subproof (e.g. '10. B @e 4}').
Nested subproofs are allowed. But a formula may only be used at a specific point if it appeared earlier in the same subproof or an outer subproof to the current subproof.

### SYMBOLS & CONVENTIONS
Use the following symbols for logical operations: 
- & = conjunction (and) 
- | = disjunction (or) 
- ~ = negation (not) 
- -> = implication (implies)
 # General Scheme for each Natural Deduction Rule
###Initial Natural Deduction Rule
Proof for <premise_1>,<premise_2>, … , <premise_n> ⊢ <conclusion_formula> must begin like: 
1. <premise_1> pre 
2. <premise_2> pre 
... 
n. <premise_n> pre
...

Then, continue to expand line by line ONLY following Natural Deduction rules.
Objective is to derive the <conclusion_formula> as the final line of the proof.

### Conjunction Introduction (&i)
- Previously: 
  m. D  
  n. B 
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p. D & B   &i  m,n

### Conjunction Elimination (&e)
- Previously: m. D & B
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  n. D   &e m
  p. B   &e m

### Implication Introduction (->i)
- Previously:
  (m ... n): subproof with D hip, ending in B 
Note: n is the last line of the subproof; m must come before n.
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p. D -> B   ->i  m-n

### Implication Elimination (->e)
- Previously:
  m. D -> B
  n. D
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p. B   ->e  m,n

### Disjunction Introduction (|i)
- Previously: m. D 
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  -> p. D | B   |i  m
OR 
- Previously: m. B 
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p. B | D   |i  m

### Disjunction Elimination (|e)
- Previously:
  m.  D | B
  (m+1 ... n): subproof with D hip, ending in C
  (n+1 ... p) subproof with B hip, ending in C
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p+1. C   |e  m, (m+1)-n, (n+1)-p

### Negation Introduction (~i)
- Previously,:
  (m+1 ... n): subproof with B hip, ending in @ (means contradiction)
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  n+1. ~B   ~i  m-n

### Negation Elimination (~e)
- Previously:
  m.  D
  n.  ~D
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  p. @   ~e  m,n

### Contradiction Elimination (@e)
Use this rule **ONLY** inside a subproof.
- Previously, lines:
  m.  @
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  n.  B   @e m

### Reductio ad Absurdum (raa)
- Previously, lines:
  (m ... n): subproof with ~D hip, ending in @ (means contradiction)
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  n+1. D   raa  m-n

### Copy Rule (copie)
- Previously:
  m.  D
- Demonstration of general application of the rule:
  n. D   copie m  Write only the final proof in <proof>...</proof> tags. Only use <proof>...</proof> tags ONCE. Only use these symbols inside the proof '&' , '->', '~', '|', '@', '(', ')', '{', '}'.
Do not include explanations, commentary, or extra text inside and outside the <proof>...</proof> tags.
 "}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "Prove '(A->B)&(~A->B) |- B', using the natural deduction method. Final answer using the notation described. "}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': ' <proof>
                        1. (A -> B) & (~A -> B) pre
                        2. A -> B &e 1
                        3. ~A -> B &e 1
                        4. { ~(A | ~A) hip
                        5. { A hip
                        6. A | ~A |i 5
                        7. @ ~e 6,4}
                        8. ~A ~i 5-7
                        9. A | ~A |i 8
                        10. @ ~e 9,4}
                        11. A | ~A raa 4-10
                        12. { A hip
                        13. B ->e 12,2}
                        14. { ~A hip
                        15. B ->e 14,3}
                        16. B |e 11, 12-13, 14-15
                        </proof>'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "Prove 'A|B |- (~A->B)&(~B->A)', using the natural deduction method. Final answer using the notation described."}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': ' <proof>

                        13. {B hip

                        16. A |e 1,11-12,13-15}
                        17. ~B -> A ->i 10-16
                        18. (~A -> B) & (~B -> A) &i 9,17
                        </proof>'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "Prove'(A->B)->A|-B->A' using the natural deduction method. Only one final answer, surrounded by <proof>...</proof>, using the notation described."}]
